notes: Windows + Cursor CLI (agent) + Ralph method (no upstream bash script). One iteration handles one story.

userStories.title: Add date and color marker before read-later article titles
userStories.description: In the read-later article list, render a date label and color marker box to the left of each article title using the same logic as the Feed article list.
acceptanceCriteria: Each read-later item shows date and color marker box before the title.
Date rendering logic matches Feed article list behavior.
Color marker logic matches Feed article list behavior.
Visual ordering is date + color marker + title from left to right.
Relevant frontend tests are updated or added, and pass.

userStories.title: Implement data-repo sync service for pull/rebase and push
userStories.description: Add a backend service that performs one data repository sync cycle in the data directory (including symlink target): detect repository, pull remote changes with rebase, and push local updates to remote when local changes exist.
acceptanceCriteria: A dedicated backend service encapsulates git sync logic for the data directory.
The service resolves data directory symlink targets before running git commands.
Sync flow includes pull with rebase from the tracking remote branch.
If local working tree changes exist, the flow stages, commits, and pushes to remote.
Failures are logged with actionable error messages and do not crash the process.

userStories.title: Run data-repo sync once at backend startup
userStories.description: Integrate the data sync service into FastAPI app startup so one sync cycle is executed during startup lifecycle before normal background jobs continue.
acceptanceCriteria: Backend startup lifecycle triggers one data sync cycle.
Startup sync invocation is wired in the existing FastAPI lifespan flow.
Startup remains resilient when sync fails (error logged, app still starts unless explicitly configured otherwise).
Relevant startup/lifecycle tests are added or updated and pass.

userStories.title: Schedule recurring data-repo sync every 30 minutes
userStories.description: Add recurring scheduling for the same data sync logic at a 30-minute interval and ensure lifecycle-safe start/stop behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: A recurring scheduler runs data sync every 30 minutes.
The scheduler starts during app startup and stops cleanly on shutdown.
The recurring job reuses the same sync service logic as startup sync.
Exceptions in scheduled runs are handled and logged without terminating future cycles.
Relevant scheduler tests are added or updated and pass.

userStories.title: Add test coverage for data sync edge cases
userStories.description: Add focused backend tests for data sync behavior covering non-repository paths, symlink paths, clean/dirty working trees, and pull/rebase conflict or command failure scenarios.
acceptanceCriteria: At least one happy-path test validates successful sync flow.
Boundary/exception tests cover non-git data directory and command failure handling.
Regression test covers a previously identified failure mode in sync orchestration.
Tests isolate git command execution via mocks or stubs and pass in CI.

userStories.title: Document data repository sync behavior and operations
userStories.description: Update project documentation for the data git synchronization mechanism, including prerequisites, trigger timings (startup + 30-minute cycle), and operational troubleshooting.
acceptanceCriteria: README and/or developer guide documents the data sync mechanism and prerequisites.
Documentation explains startup trigger and 30-minute recurring trigger clearly.
Documentation includes failure-handling and troubleshooting notes for git auth/conflicts.
Docs are consistent with implemented behavior and reviewed for accuracy.
